title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to IoT technology: Definitions and Characteristics of IoT, IoT
Architectural View, Physical Design of IOT, Logical Design of IoT- IoT
Functional
blocks,
IoT
communication
models,
IoT
Enabling
Technologies, IoT Levels & Deployment Templates.

title: Module 2
content:
Internet of things in healthcare: Innovative IoT applications in healthcare-
Sensors and smart devices, biosensors, patient health portals, Machine
learning applications, Blockchain- based initiatives.
Internet of health things and artificial intelligence: The architecture of
healthcare IoT, Kaa-a leading platform for state-of-the-art medical IoT.

title: Module 3
content:
Internet of Things Technologies: IoT in biomedical engineering,
IoMT system architecture, Components of system architecture.
Some other use cases of IoT in the healthcare industry: Reducing waiting
time in case of emergency, Enhanced medicine management, and Smart
beds for patients.

title: Module 4
content:
IoT based image-guided surgery: Role of new-age technologies in clinical
surgery, Proposed model- architecture.
Medical big data mining and processing in e-healthcare: Introduction of
Big data sources, Big data mining and processing.
Odontogenic tumors: Prevalence and Demographic distribution through
IoT.
